threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still say 'the Tube' instead of just 'public transport' while waving your contactless like it's a magic wand.
Last night on the 36, a couple argued over a missing shoe—turns out it was under the seat the whole time. Classic Peckham chaos.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I found a guy selling fresh falafel from a bike cart while a street artist painted a mural of a cat on a nearby wall. That's Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg will you find a kebab shop that serves beer brewed on-site. Nothing like a late-night döner with a local brew to end the night!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local by their knee-jerk reaction to BART delays—rolling eyes and a resigned sigh. Tourists just look confused.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a guy singing opera on the 31 and the whole bus just went quiet. Two stops later, he had the entire bus clapping. Only in SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think every plaza is for gathering, but locals know the quietest spots behind the trees are where the real conversations happen.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by the way they still hesitate at street crossings, waiting for the green man even when traffic is sparse.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it 'the Tube' and not 'the Overground' when you hop from Dalston Junction to Liverpool Street. That’ll give you away every time.
Caught the N87 last Saturday, and a guy was passionately trying to convince a stranger that pineapple on pizza is a crime. Only in Brixton!

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a street musician playing a violin while a kid did breakdancing on the sidewalk, and everyone just stopped to watch. That’s Neukölln for you.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain will you find a local cafe that serves coffee out of vintage gas station pumps. It's quirky and oddly nostalgic.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell who's local by their disdain for BART - they know to plan for delays like it's a second job. Tourists just look confused at the schedule.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy serenading the 38 bus with a ukulele today while waiting for his ride. Only in SF do you get live music with your transit delays.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about the boulevards, but the real charm hides in the tiny squares where locals gather for vermut and gossip.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They’re the ones still fumbling with their metro map, standing at the wrong exit while everyone else knows to look for the escalators.
